introSection: Kondaa is a steel roller coaster at Walibi Belgium in Wavre, Walloon Brabant, Wallonia, Belgium. Built by Intamin, the ride opened on 8 May 2021.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride was first announced at the 2018 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA, alongside Toutatis, the Intamin multi-launch coaster opening at Parc Asterix in 2023.

In the summer of 2020, the first track pieces arrived at the park.

The name Kondaa was announced in November 2020.

Kondaa opened to guests on 8 May 2021.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Kondaa is a Mega Coaster-style ride from Intamin. It is tall and  long.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: After climbing an initial chain lift hill, the ride begins with an 80-degree twisted drop similar to the one on Expedition GeForce at Plopsaland Deutschland. A tall hill follows, then an RMC-esque outerbank to the right. The non inverting cobra roll, described as the "first of its kind" by Intamin, comes next. A low-to-the ground wave turn and hill follows, then a twisted double down. The ride ends with more bunny hills and an over-banked curve before roaring into the brakes.

fullText:
Kondaa is a steel roller coaster at Walibi Belgium in Wavre, Walloon Brabant, Wallonia, Belgium. Built by Intamin, the ride opened on 8 May 2021.

The ride was first announced at the 2018 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA, alongside Toutatis, the Intamin multi-launch coaster opening at Parc Asterix in 2023.

In the summer of 2020, the first track pieces arrived at the park.

The name Kondaa was announced in November 2020.

Kondaa opened to guests on 8 May 2021.

Kondaa is a Mega Coaster-style ride from Intamin. It is tall and  long.

After climbing an initial chain lift hill, the ride begins with an 80-degree twisted drop similar to the one on Expedition GeForce at Plopsaland Deutschland. A tall hill follows, then an RMC-esque outerbank to the right. The non inverting cobra roll, described as the "first of its kind" by Intamin, comes next. A low-to-the ground wave turn and hill follows, then a twisted double down. The ride ends with more bunny hills and an over-banked curve before roaring into the brakes.
